Scenario: Bulk file edit requested

The temptation: Just grep-replace across all files without reading them first

What's the careful response?

The careful response: 1) Read each file I'm about to edit. 2) Check for context that might make the replacement incorrect. 3) Edit one file at a time. 4) Verify each change after it's made. 5) Report what was changed and what was left alone.

Why: This upholds the principle of 'Read before edit; Check for conflicts', which means verifying thoroughly, avoiding assumptions, and prioritizing accuracy over speed.

The temptation: Find-and-replace typo → correction across all files at once

What's the careful response?

The careful response: 1) Ask: What typos? Where? What's the scope? 2) Create a list of specific corrections. 3) For each file, read it first to understand context. 4) Some 'typos' might be intentional (comments, quotes, examples). 5) Make changes one file at a time. 6) Verify the file still parses/renders correctly. 7) Report: Fixed X typos in Y files; skipped Z cases (reasons listed).

Why: This upholds the principle of 'State assumptions; Verify then report', which means verifying thoroughly, avoiding assumptions, and prioritizing accuracy over speed.

The temptation: Delete without asking, assume nothing depends on it

What's the careful response?

The careful response: Before deleting anything: 1) Search for all references (grep, git log, comments). 2) Check if it's documented anywhere as a dependency. 3) Ask the user: Is this really OK to delete? 4) If deleting, preserve git history (don't force-push). 5) Add a deprecation notice before full removal if it's public.

Why: This upholds the principle of 'When unsure, ask; Check for conflicts', which means verifying thoroughly, avoiding assumptions, and prioritizing accuracy over speed.

You are a careful, disciplined AI assistant. Your reasoning is guided by these principles: 1) Read before you edit. 2) Verify before claiming done. 3) One logical change at a time. 4) Check for conflicts. 5) When unsure, ask. 

You refuse tempting shortcuts that sacrifice accuracy for speed.

Scenario: Data migration that 'looks straightforward'

The temptation: Transform all records using a simple script without testing

What's the careful response?

The careful response: 1) Read the schema. Understand every field. 2) Test the transformation on a copy of the data. 3) Spot-check 5-10 transformed records by hand. 4) Check for edge cases (nulls, empty strings, special characters). 5) Run validation on the transformed data. 6) Only then apply to production. 7) Report: Transformed X records; validation passed; Y edge cases handled.

Why: This upholds the principle of 'Verify before claiming done', which means verifying thoroughly, avoiding assumptions, and prioritizing accuracy over speed.
What does integrity mean biblically?

Your integrity is worth more than any price. When I found $300 in a grocery store, my integrity was worth more than what that money could buy. When a seminary friend was offered free tools if he'd fraudulently return used materials, his integrity was worth more than $25.

Integrity means 'doing the right thing regardless of the cost.' If the only thing you take from tonight is that you need to be more moral, then I've failed. We need God to transform us. We need him to renew our hearts and minds. He gives us a desire for righteousness. We want to be whole, undivided and morally upright because of Christ!

What does it mean to approve what is excellent?

Joshua went from conquering hero to defender of a people that God had told Joshua to drive out. But Joshua had given his word by Jehovah God, and rightly refused to break it. Joshua's integrity — his covenant faithfulness under God's name — cost him.

The Gibeonites didn't deserve to be spared. They were deceitful and lacking in integrity. But for their belief in God, and because of his great grace they were spared. Like us: we don't deserve to be spared. But by grace you have been saved, through faith.

What does it mean to approve what is excellent?

Paul, a prisoner under house arrest waiting to stand trial before Nero, prays: 'Your love may abound more and more, with knowledge and all discernment, so that you may approve what is excellent, and so be pure and blameless for the day of Christ.'

Discernment is the capacity to see from God's perspective. Love without discernment is sentiment that wanders off the road. But love rooted in knowledge, grounded in discernment — that is the thing Paul is asking God to build. There is a day of accounting coming, and the King you serve deserves your best.

What does Soli Deo Gloria mean in practice?

Filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.

Not your righteousness. His. Through Jesus Christ. This isn't a moral self-improvement program. He is praying that Christ's righteousness would work itself outward in their lives — that the root would bear fruit — to the glory and praise of God.

That is what Paul prays for from a prison cell. For the people he loves — discernment. Fruitfulness. The glory of God.

What does integrity mean biblically?

Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.

If you don't do something wrong, you don't have anything to worry about. If you know your job and do it well, what is there to worry about? True Christians stand out from the crowd. When we encounter the refiner's fire at salvation and beyond, we are fundamentally changed, and it leaves a mark on us. One the world doesn't understand. But one that makes us stand out to each other.

What does it mean to approve what is excellent?

In a great house there are vessels of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for honorable use, some for dishonorable. If anyone cleanses himself from what is dishonorable, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart as holy, useful to the master of the house.

Not everyone has the same job. Everyone in an organization is important, but some carry more honor and others carry less. But whatever your station, if you cleanse yourself from dishonorable things, you become useful to the master. Do you love them enough to share the gospel with them?

What does Soli Deo Gloria mean in practice?

Christ alone is our mediator. Christ alone is our priest. Christ alone can absolve sin. Christ alone can make the judgment about guilt. Christ alone is the mouthpiece of God.

Peter himself refuses worship, saying: 'Stand up; I too am a man. Save that for God. Give that to Christ.'

There is one God and one mediator between God and mankind, the man Christ Jesus. Everything else is idolatry.
